chunk_summary: This section outlines donor milk eligibility tiers, reassessment intervals, and exit criteria to optimize DHM allocation in NICUs, focusing on preterm and high-risk infants.
learning_objectives: Understand the tiered eligibility system for donor human milk (DHM) allocation in NICUs.
Learn the reassessment criteria and intervals for DHM eligibility.
Identify exit criteria for transitioning from DHM to maternal milk or formula.
Recognize the clinical markers for prioritizing DHM use in preterm and high-risk infants.
key_facts: Tier 1 includes extremely preterm (<28 weeks) or ELBW (<1000 g) infants, infants with NEC, post-surgery, or high-risk clinical instability.
Tier 2 includes stable very preterm (<32 weeks) or VLBW (<1500 g) infants with inadequate maternal milk supply.
Reassessment of DHM eligibility should occur every 24–48 hours, documenting maternal milk volume, infant tolerance, and clinical risk.
Exit criteria include sustained maternal milk availability (>80% of enteral volume for 48 hours) or clinical maturity (34–36 weeks PMA).
Transition from DHM should be stepwise, prioritizing maternal milk first, with formula as a secondary option.
reference_quote: Donor human milk for preventing necrotising enterocolitis in very preterm or very low birth weight infants — https://www.cochranelibrary.com/cdsr/doi/...
document_key_topics: NICU feeding protocols for preterm infants
